authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Customs Entry and Filing Framework
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Department of Homeland Security, and U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary regulatory rules for customs entry scope, filing, and customs-processing governance; defines where evidence and documents are required before progression.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Initial documentation intake and entry-filing control

authoritative_sources.title: CBP Import and Entry Processing Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Operational guide for how trade filing systems and processing stages are managed and where customs-documentation failures are handled.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Workflow sequencing and system retrieval checks

authoritative_sources.title: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network Statutes and Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, FinCEN
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary baseline for KYC/AML risk boundaries and record expectations when fraud or suspicious transaction indicators appear in trade documentation workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Consumer Financial Protection in Billing and Dispute Communication
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Consumer-impact and disclosure expectations that constrain how unresolved billing, charges, or financial consequences are communicated downstream.

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC Information Security and Fraud Risk Resources
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Operational risk and reconciliation expectations referenced to determine when finance-side systems are sufficiently reliable for confident control decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: NACHA Operating Rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines reconciliation and dispute handling expectations for payment events that influence release and documentation completeness in trade finance pathways.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 20022 Financial Services Messaging
authoritative_sources.source_class: Industry terminology and record normalization source
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry-standard message and field naming for financial and trade documentation integration; reduces ambiguity in cross-system mappings.

authoritative_sources.title: NAICS Industry Classification Reference
authoritative_sources.source_class: Industry terminology and record normalization source
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAICS taxonomy anchors industry-specific artifacts and prevents drift into non-specific quality-control behavior.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and record-of-truth verification
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether account, policy, transaction, and shipment inputs are sufficient and identify required artifacts before any hold/release recommendation.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
Industry terminology and record normalization source

workflow_stages.name: Documentation integrity and evidence mapping
workflow_stages.description: Validate invoices, commercial documents, customs filing artifacts, and approval evidence against hold criteria and record ownership.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Control and hold/release enforcement
workflow_stages.description: Apply control stages, hold indicators, and approval dependencies to produce a release or escalation decision with explicit blockers.

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, AML, consumer-impact escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when fraud indicators, AML flags, or customer impact conflicts require adjacent specialist or human review.

workflow_stages.name: Conflict resolution and tenant adaptation
workflow_stages.description: When public guidance and tenant policy disagree, return explicit conflict outputs and defer final decisions until policy precedence is confirmed.
source_classes: Industry terminology and record normalization source
Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

artifacts: Shipment, invoice, packing list, and customs-document set
Filing and release artifacts from customs/workflow systems
Reconciliation outputs (GL, policy, and payment systems)
systems_of_record: Claims, policy administration, and servicing systems

allowed: Produce in-scope quality/safety recommendations for documentation readiness and hold/release gatekeeping.
Validate evidence sufficiency, reconciliation consistency, and disclosure dependencies before recommending progression.
Escalate when fraud/AML or consumer harm risk appears and map evidence to adjacent owner or formal review lanes.
escalate: Missing or contradictory records, source conflicts, or stale records affect outcome.
Fraud, AML, or consumer-dispute indicators exceed lane authority.
Disclosure/reconciliation differences require formal adjudication or owner confirmation.
refuse: Requests to bypass hold/release checks or override policy without approved authority.
Requests that require legal interpretation, licensing, or formal QA finality.
Requests to certify completion when required artifacts or approvals are missing.

domain_failure_modes: Clearing hold/release without complete customs filing artifacts.
Ignoring reconciliation differences and treating unresolved financial evidence as acceptable.
Routing fraud or consumer-impact cases without formal escalation and owner handoff.
Applying public defaults when tenant policy requires stricter trade-specific evidence thresholds.
Conflating record truth between policy systems and case systems without explicit source precedence.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Source precedence between policy and case systems for hold state
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant policy is mandatory when it is stricter than public workflow references; otherwise escalate if conflict cannot be resolved safely.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Threshold that triggers formal QA escalation versus general specialist escalation
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Require explicit tenant routing policy and owner map; if absent, route as low-confidence escalation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific reconciliation normalizing of shipment value versus billed value
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Default to hard-block with mismatch evidence summary until tenant approval policy clarifies accepted tolerance.

notes: The research pack combines customs workflow, payment/reconciliation, AML, and consumer-impact sources to support trade-specific quality-safety behavior.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; all open points are converted to explicit escalation or return conditions.
